There is no built-in `hidden` flag for block titles.

The title block renders unconditionally before link filtering occurs. Without code changes, remove the title block or delete the entire section. Hiding the heading while keeping links visible is not supported by the current setup.

Empty link-row divs may still render when all links in a row are hidden.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Empty group titles cannot be hidden at initialization.

`| hidden` only hides links, not block titles. The heading still renders because `block.html` always outputs it. To remove the title, delete the entire block or move its rows elsewhere. Programmatic support would require adding `hidden: true` to the block YAML or modifying `block.html` to skip empty headings.
</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Group titles (`- title: "..."`) don't support `| hidden` — that flag only works on individual links in a `row`.

In your file, both links under "Board Game Stores" are already hidden:

```71:75:content/board-games.md
  - title: "Board Game Stores"
    row:
      - "Simple Game | https://shopee.ph/kinkong.ph?page=0&sortBy=ctime&tab=0 | hidden"
  - row:
      - "Houmi Game | https://shopee.ph/houmi.ph?page=0&sortBy=ctime&tab=0 | hidden"
```

The heading still shows because `block.html` always renders the `<h3>` before it filters hidden links:

```4:6:layouts/partials/block.html
{{- if $block.title -}}
  <h3 class="group-title">{{ $block.title }}</h3>
{{- end -}}
```

**What you can do today (content only):**

1. **Remove the title block** — delete the `- title: "Board Game Stores"` line and keep the rows under "Manila Stores" (or delete those rows too if you don't need the links at all).
2. **Delete the whole block** — if every link is hidden and you don't use `disableHiding: true` in `hugo.toml`, lines 71–75 can go entirely.

**Example** — fold into Manila Stores without a separate heading:

```yaml
  - title: "Manila Stores"
    row:
      - "GamingLib | https://www.gaminglib.com/collections/all?sort_by=created-descending"
  - row:
      - "Geekbox PH | https://geekboxph.com/collections/shop-all?sort_by=created-descending"
      - "Shopee Store | https://shopee.ph/geekboxph?page=0&sortBy=ctime"
  - row:
      - "Board Game Essentials | https://shopee.ph/boardgameessentials?page=0&sortBy=ctime | hidden"
      - "Simple Game | https://shopee.ph/kinkong.ph?page=0&sortBy=ctime&tab=0 | hidden"
  - row:
      - "Houmi Game | https://shopee.ph/houmi.ph?page=0&sortBy=ctime&tab=0 | hidden"
```

There is no `hidden` field for group titles in the YAML schema right now. If you want the template to skip a title when all its links are hidden (or to support something like `hidden: true` on a title block), that would be a small change to `block.html`. I can add that if you want.

---

_**User**_

yes

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Implementing the template change to skip group titles when all links under them are hidden, or to support `hidden: true` on title blocks.

Planning the minimal fix in `block.html`. The title will only render if at least one visible link exists below it, or if `showAll` is true. Each block is self-contained and may contain a title, from group, row, or label.

Realized that titles paired with a row belong only to that block. When a title stands alone, the following row block contains the links. This clarifies how to check visibility across separate blocks.

Confirmed that separate blocks remain independent in the HTML. A title paired with a row belongs only to that block. A following row without a title renders separately, not grouped under the previous heading.

Search.js groups consecutive link rows under a heading, even when they appear in separate Hugo blocks. This means a title paired with a hidden row still affects search results. The template fix must account for this grouping, either by checking the current block's visible links or by scanning subsequent rows until the next title.

Settled on the exact condition for `block.html`: render the title only when the current block itself contains at least one visible item. This handles both title-plus-row blocks and subsequent row-only blocks that search.js groups under the previous heading.

Checking only the current block's visible links is insufficient. A title paired with a hidden row still leaves the following row block under the same group heading. The template fix must account for subsequent rows too.

JavaScript could hide titles after page load, but post-processing in section.html is too complex. Counting visible links inside
</details></think>
